skill.name: quant-analyst
skill.description: Build financial models, backtest trading strategies, and analyze market data. Implements risk metrics, portfolio optimization, and statistical arbitrage.
skill.summary: Quantitative analyst for algorithmic trading and financial modeling
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code. The static analyzer found 0 files scanned and 0 potential security issues. The skill provides guidance for quantitative finance tasks using pandas, numpy, and scipy without making any system-level operations. No dangerous patterns detected.

content.user_title: Build Trading Strategies and Financial Models
content.value_statement: Develop and backtest algorithmic trading strategies with risk metrics and portfolio optimization. Get actionable insights for quantitative finance without requiring deep expertise in financial modeling.
actual_capabilities: Develop and backtest trading strategies with transaction costs and slippage modeling
Calculate risk metrics including VaR, Sharpe ratio, and maximum drawdown
Perform portfolio optimization using Markowitz and Black-Litterman models
Execute time series analysis and forecasting for market data
Implement options pricing and Greeks calculation
Build statistical arbitrage and pairs trading strategies
limitations: Does not execute live trades or connect to brokerage APIs
Does not provide real-time market data or price feeds
Does not guarantee profitability or accurate predictions
Requires user to validate all models before production use

use_cases.title: Strategy Development
use_cases.description: Create and backtest new trading strategies with realistic market simulation

use_cases.title: Risk Assessment
use_cases.description: Evaluate portfolio risk exposure and calculate key risk metrics

use_cases.title: Portfolio Optimization
use_cases.description: Optimize asset allocation using modern portfolio theory

prompt_templates.title: Basic Strategy Backtest
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me backtest a trading strategy. I want to test a [moving average crossover strategy] on [AAPL] stock with data from [2020-2024]. Include transaction costs of [0.1%] and slippage of [0.05%]. Calculate Sharpe ratio, maximum drawdown, and total return.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick backtest of a simple strategy

prompt_templates.title: Risk Metrics Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate the following risk metrics for my portfolio [SPY 60%, AGG 40%]: Value at Risk (VaR) at 95% confidence, Expected Shortfall, Sharpe ratio, and maximum drawdown over the last 3 years. Use historical simulation method.

prompt_templates.title: Portfolio Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform Markowitz mean-variance optimization for my portfolio with these assets: [AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, JNJ, XOM]. Use 5 years of historical data. Maximize Sharpe ratio with a target return of [8%]. Include the efficient frontier in your analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Pairs Trading Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a statistical arbitrage pairs trading strategy between [KO and PEP] using the last 2 years of daily data. Include cointegration testing, hedge ratio calculation, entry/exit signals with z-score thresholds, and backtest results with performance metrics.

output_examples.input: Backtest a momentum strategy on tech stocks
output_examples.output:
Strategy: Momentum with 12-month lookback, 1-month holding period

Backtest Results (2019-2024):
- Total Return: 124.5% (vs S&P 500: 89.2%)
- Sharpe Ratio: 1.42
- Max Drawdown: -18.3%
- Win Rate: 58%
- Avg Trade: 2.1%

Risk Metrics:
- VaR (95%): -4.2%
- Volatility: 15.8%

Note: Past performance does not guarantee future results.

output_examples.input: Optimize my portfolio for maximum Sharpe ratio
output_examples.output:
Optimal Portfolio Allocation:
- AAPL: 25%
- MSFT: 22%
- GOOGL: 18%
- AMZN: 15%
- NVDA: 12%
- JNJ: 8%

Expected Metrics:
- Expected Return: 14.2%
- Expected Volatility: 18.5%
- Sharpe Ratio: 0.68

Efficient Frontier: Available for review
Rebalancing Recommendation: Quarterly

best_practices: Always use out-of-sample testing to validate strategies before production deployment
Include realistic transaction costs, slippage, and market impact in all backtests
Focus on risk-adjusted returns (Sharpe ratio) rather than absolute returns
anti_patterns: Do not overfit strategies to historical data without proper cross-validation
Avoid using future information (look-ahead bias) in backtesting
Never skip the separation between research and production code environments

faq.question: Can this skill execute live trades?
faq.answer: No, this skill only provides strategy development and analysis. It does not connect to brokerages or execute trades.

faq.question: What data sources does it use?
faq.answer: The skill works with data you provide. You can use Yahoo Finance, FRED, or any data source you have access to.

faq.question: Is the backtesting realistic?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes transaction costs, slippage, and market microstructure assumptions. You can customize these parameters.

faq.question: Can it handle options strategies?
faq.answer: Yes, it supports options pricing, Greeks calculation, and options-based strategy analysis.

faq.question: Does it support machine learning models?
faq.answer: The skill can help you implement and backtest ML-based strategies using sklearn and other libraries.

faq.question: How do I validate a strategy before using it?
faq.answer: Use out-of-sample testing, walk-forward analysis, and paper trading. Never deploy a strategy without thorough validation.
